title: geofencing — field classification
description: Location coordinates + accuracy are PERSONAL DATA. Subject id + override reason are internal. Every override reason may reference a specific worker's absence, so keep restricted.

restricted: GeofenceCheck.captured_location — precise WGS84 coordinates. Personal data under GDPR.
GeofenceCheck.override_reason — may reference specific worker conduct or excused absences (e.g., 'I was inside the building but my phone GPS drifted').
regulated_minor: ANY GeofenceCheck row for a subject_type='attendance' or 'attendance_submission' when the linked athlete is a minor — the entire row inherits regulated_minor classification, enforced by the consumer module's attendance policy.
regulated_financial: GeofenceCheck rows where subject_type='staff_clockin' — constitute wage-and-hour records under FLSA.
audit_log: audit_log.geofence.* rows for override applied + retention purge actions

notes: captured_location is stored as a Magellan Point at SRID 4326 (WGS84). Precision is bit-for-bit what the client submitted; we do NOT round or bucket coordinates.
The workspace admin surface truncates coordinates to 3 decimal places (~110 m) in list views. Precise coords available only in the single-check detail view + gated to compliance role.
For minor-subject rows, the frontend renders 'Location captured' rather than the map — the coordinates are still queryable server-side by audit-reviewers.
